message:
Add indexer orchestration scaffolding

Establish indexer module structure with placeholder mod.rs for future
index coordination and update management implementation.

Module purpose (per PLAN § 3.1, § 8):
The indexer orchestrates the multi-step process of extracting conversation
data from agent-specific sources (via connectors), persisting normalized
data to SQLite (via storage layer), and building/maintaining the Tantivy
full-text index (via search layer). It coordinates concurrent scanning,
filesystem watching for incremental updates, and batch write scheduling
to balance indexing throughput with query availability.

Planned mod.rs responsibilities:

Initial scan orchestration (PLAN § 8.1):
- Detect installed agents via connector detection APIs, display found
  agents to user in TUI dialog: "Detected: Codex, Cline, Gemini. Index
  now? [Yes] [Skip]" with checkbox list of agents to enable/disable
  before scan
- Kick off full scan in background thread (tokio::spawn), iterate over
  enabled connectors calling scan_sessions() for each, accumulate
  Vec<Conversation> results across all agents
- Show progress bar in TUI status bar updating as each connector reports
  progress: "Indexing Codex: 327/1024 sessions…" via indicatif or
  custom progress reporting channel
- Batch insert conversations to SQLite in transactions (1000 conversations
  per commit for performance per PLAN § 4.3), then bulk add to Tantivy
  index (commit every 10k messages to balance freshness vs throughput
  per PLAN § 5.2)
- Track scan metadata: last_scan_timestamp, indexed_file_count per agent
  in SQLite meta table for incremental scan starting point

Incremental update management (PLAN § 8.2):
- For log-file-based connectors (Codex, Claude, Gemini, Cline):
  - Initialize notify::Watcher on each connector's history root directory
    (e.g., ~/.codex/sessions, ~/.claude/projects, ~/.gemini/tmp, VS Code
    globalStorage)
  - Debounce filesystem events (notify::DebouncedEvent with 2-second
    delay) to avoid thrashing when files are written incrementally (e.g.,
    Codex appending to active session rollout JSONL)
  - On new/modified file event, delegate to connector scan_sessions with
    since_timestamp parameter = last indexed timestamp for that source
    file, connector returns only new/changed conversations
  - Upsert to SQLite (INSERT OR REPLACE on UNIQUE constraint), update
    affected Tantivy documents (delete by message_id term query, re-add
    with fresh content)

- For SQLite-based connectors (OpenCode):
  - Periodic polling (every 60 seconds via tokio::time::interval) for
    database file mtime change compared to last indexed mtime
  - On change, open database read-only, query for rows newer than
    last_imported_row_id or last_imported_timestamp tracked in meta
    table, scan new rows only
  - Same upsert + Tantivy update flow as file-based sources

- Filesystem watcher resource management:
  - Use crossbeam-channel bounded queue (capacity 1000) for IndexUpdate
    messages from watchers to indexer worker thread to prevent unbounded
    memory growth if indexing can't keep up with file creation rate
  - Apply backpressure: if queue full, drop oldest events and log warning
    "Indexing falling behind, consider full rescan" to prevent OOM
  - On graceful shutdown (Ctrl+C via tokio::signal::ctrl_c()), drain
    queue, commit pending Tantivy changes, close SQLite connection

Schema migration coordination (PLAN § 8.3):
- On startup, check SQLite meta table for schema_version (e.g., version 1)
  vs current binary schema version (hardcoded SCHEMA_VERSION constant)
- If mismatch (upgrade or downgrade):
  - Run migration functions: up_v1_to_v2(), down_v2_to_v1() that execute
    ALTER TABLE statements or data transformations
  - Rebuild Tantivy index from SQLite if search schema changed (fields
    added/removed/renamed), delete old index directory, create new with
    version suffix (index/v2/), full scan from conversations table
  - Update meta.schema_version, commit transaction
- If migration fails, display error and refuse to start (data corruption
  risk if schema mismatched)

Threading model (PLAN § 3.2):
- Main thread: tokio async runtime runs UI event loop (crossterm input
  polling, Ratatui rendering)
- Indexer thread: dedicated tokio task receives IndexCommand messages
  (FullReindex, IncrementalScan, FilesystemEvent) via crossbeam channel,
  dispatches to connector/storage/search layers sequentially per command
- Search worker pool: rayon::ThreadPool (size = num_cpus) for parallel
  Tantivy query execution and result post-processing, sends SearchResult
  messages back to UI thread via channel

Channel definitions:
```rust
enum IndexCommand {
    FullReindex { agents: Vec<String> },
    IncrementalScan { agent: String, paths: Vec<PathBuf> },
    FilesystemEvent { agent: String, event: notify::Event },
    Shutdown,
}

enum UiEvent {
    Key(crossterm::event::KeyEvent),
    Tick,  // for periodic UI refresh
    SearchResult(Vec<ConversationSearchHit>),
    IndexProgress { agent: String, current: usize, total: usize },
}
```

Error recovery strategies:
- Connector parse failures: log error with source file path, continue to
  next file (don't let one malformed JSONL crash entire scan)
- SQLite transaction conflicts: retry with exponential backoff (unlikely
  with WAL mode allowing concurrent reads, but possible if multiple
  processes access database)
- Tantivy index corruption: detect via IndexReader::acquire_searcher()
  failure at startup, offer user choice to delete index and rebuild from
  SQLite or exit (SQLite is source of truth, index is derived)
- Filesystem watcher failures (e.g., inotify limit exceeded on Linux):
  fall back to periodic polling of last-modified timestamps for watched
  directories, log warning recommending fs.inotify.max_user_watches
  increase

This commit contains only the module declaration (mod.rs) with placeholder
comment to establish the indexer/ namespace per implementation checklist
(PLAN § 14.7: index orchestration after connectors, storage, and search
provide their APIs). Actual scan loop, progress reporting, and watch
management will be implemented in subsequent commit after minimal TUI
demonstrates progress bar integration and connectors demonstrate
scan_sessions API shape.

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 3.1 (indexer
component), § 3.2 (threading model), § 8 (indexer/sync flow), Cargo.toml
(notify, crossbeam-channel, rayon dependencies), README.md (indexer/
module description).
